messages.content: I will check if QQ Music is installed and then attempt to open it and start playback using AppleScript.

thoughts.subject: Initiating Music Playback
thoughts.description: I've determined the user's intent: play music via QQ Music. I'm focusing on the macOS environment I'm in, and I'll use the `open` command to launch the application. I am now confirming QQ Music's application name or bundle identifier to ensure correct execution.

thoughts.subject: Investigating Application Launch
thoughts.description: I am now verifying the existence of QQ Music on this system. I plan to use the `open` command to launch the application if found. I'm prioritizing direct app launch first. AppleScript for playback will be the next step, using `tell application "QQMusic" to play`, after launch verification.

toolCalls.name: run_shell_command

args.description: Locating QQ Music application on macOS.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

messages.content: I'll search for the application directly in the `/Applications` folder.

messages.content: I'll open QQ Music and then use AppleScript to start playback.

thoughts.subject: Locating the Application
thoughts.description: I've pinpointed the QQMusic application's location on the system – it resides in `/Applications/QQMusic.app`. My next step involves leveraging AppleScript to directly command the app to initiate playback. This seems like a straightforward approach for programmatically controlling its behavior.

toolCalls.name: run_shell_command

args.description: Opening QQ Music and sending a play command (Space key).
args.command: open -a "QQMusic" && osascript -e 'tell application "QQMusic" to activate' -e 'delay 1' -e 'tell application "System Events" to key code 49'

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. messages.content: 我已经为你打开了 QQ 音乐并尝试发送了播放指令。

thoughts.subject: Evaluating the Command Execution
thoughts.description: I've just confirmed the AppleScript command execution. The expected key press (code 49, spacebar) has been triggered, thus the playback command should have been sent to QQ Music. I'll provide feedback to the user now.
